info.title: dotnet-csharpier-config
info.description: CSharpier is an not opinionated code formatter for c#.

options.name: check
options.description: Check that files are formatted. Will not write any changes.

options.name: loglevel
options.description: Specify the log level - Debug, Information (default), Warning, Error, None

options.name: no-cache
options.description: Bypass the cache to determine if a file needs to be formatted.

options.name: no-msbuild-check
options.description: Bypass the check to determine if a csproj files references a different version of CSharpier.MsBuild.

options.name: include-generated
options.description: Include files generated by the SDK and files that begin with <autogenerated /> comments

options.name: fast
options.description: Skip comparing syntax tree of formatted file to original file to validate changes.

options.name: skip-write
options.description: Skip writing changes. Generally used for testing to ensure csharpier doesn't throw any errors or cause syntax tree validation failures.

options.name: write-stdout
options.description: Write the results of formatting any files to stdout.

options.name: pipe-multiple-files
options.description: Keep csharpier running so that multiples files can be piped to it via stdin.

options.name: server
options.description: Run CSharpier as a server so that multiple files may be formatted.

options.name: server-port
options.description: Specify the port that CSharpier should start on. Defaults to a random unused port.

options.name: config-path
options.description: Path to the CSharpier configuration file

options.name: compilation-errors-as-warnings
options.description: Treat compilation errors from files as warnings instead of errors.

arguments.name: directoryOrFile
arguments.description: One or more paths to a directory containing C# files to format or a C# file to format. It may be ommited when piping data via stdin.
